briefdescription.para: The set is an ad-hoc set.
The set is a CSIP Coordinated Set.
Type of CAP set.
Callback function for bt_cap_unicast_group_foreach_stream()
Callback function for bt_cap_initiator_broadcast_foreach_stream()
Register the Common Audio Service.

detaileddescription.para: This will register and enable the service and make it discoverable by clients. This will also register a Coordinated Set Identification Service instance.
This shall only be done as a server, and requires BT_CAP_ACCEPTOR_SET_MEMBER. If BT_CAP_ACCEPTOR_SET_MEMBER is not enabled, the Common Audio Service will by statically registered.

parameterdescription.para: Coordinated Set Identification Service register parameters.
Pointer to the registered Coordinated Set Identification Service.

simplesect.para: 0 if success, errno on failure.

briefdescription.para: Discovers audio support on a remote device.

detaileddescription.para: This will discover the Common Audio Service (CAS) on the remote device, to verify if the remote device supports the Common Audio Profile.

parameterdescription.para: Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Register Audio operations for a Common Audio Profile stream.

detaileddescription.para: Register Audio operations for a stream.

briefdescription.para: Send data to Common Audio Profile stream without timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: See bt_bap_stream_send() for more information

simplesect.para: Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_cap_stream *stream, struct net_buf *buf, uint16_t seq_num, uint32_t ts)
memberdef.name: bt_cap_stream_send_ts

briefdescription.para: Send data to Common Audio Profile stream with timestamp.

detaileddescription.para: See bt_bap_stream_send() for more information

simplesect.para: Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

parameterdescription.para: Buffer containing data to be sent.
Packet Sequence number. This value shall be incremented for each call to this function and at least once per SDU interval for a specific channel.
Timestamp of the SDU in microseconds (us). This value can be used to transmit multiple SDUs in the same SDU interval in a CIG or BIG.

briefdescription.para: Get ISO transmission timing info for a Common Audio Profile stream.

detaileddescription.para: See bt_bap_stream_get_tx_sync() for more information

simplesect.para: Support for sending must be supported, determined by CONFIG_BT_AUDIO_TX.

briefdescription.para: Create unicast group.

detaileddescription.para: Create a new audio unicast group with one or more audio streams as a unicast client. All streams shall share the same framing. All streams in the same direction shall share the same interval and latency (see bt_bap_qos_cfg).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the unicast group created.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Reconfigure unicast group.

detaileddescription.para: Reconfigure a unicast group with one or more audio streams as a unicast client. All streams shall share the same framing. All streams in the same direction shall share the same interval and latency (see bt_bap_qos_cfg). All streams in param shall already belong to unicast_group. Use bt_cap_unicast_group_add_streams() to add additional streams.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the unicast group created.

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_cap_unicast_group *unicast_group, const struct bt_cap_unicast_group_stream_pair_param params[], size_t num_param)
memberdef.name: bt_cap_unicast_group_add_streams

briefdescription.para: Add streams to a unicast group as a unicast client.

detaileddescription.para: This function can be used to add additional streams to a bt_cap_unicast_group.

This can be called at any time before any of the streams in the group has been started (see bt_bap_stream_ops.started()). This can also be called after the streams have been stopped (see bt_bap_stream_ops.stopped()).

Once a stream has been added to a unicast group, it cannot be removed. To remove a stream from a group, the group must be deleted with bt_cap_unicast_group_delete(), but this will require all streams in the group to be released first.

parameterdescription.para: Array of stream parameters with streams being added to the group.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Delete audio unicast group.

detaileddescription.para: Delete a audio unicast group as a client. All streams in the group shall be in the idle or configured state.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the unicast group to delete

simplesect.para: Zero on success or (negative) error code otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Iterate through all streams in a unicast group.

parameterdescription.para: User specified data that is sent to the callback function
Success (even if no streams exists in the group).
The func returned false and stopped the iteration.

briefdescription.para: Return structure holding information of unicast group.

parameterdescription.para: The structure object to be filled with the info.

briefdescription.para: Register Common Audio Profile Initiator callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure. Shall remain static.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Unregister Common Audio Profile Initiator callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure that was previously registered.
cb is NULL or cb was not registered

briefdescription.para: Setup and start unicast audio streams for a set of devices.

detaileddescription.para: The result of this operation is that the streams in param will be initialized and will be usable for streaming audio data. The unicast_group value can be used to update and stop the streams.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_UNICAST_CLIENT must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters to start the audio streams.
if a CAP procedure is already in progress
All streams are already in the streaming state

briefdescription.para: Update unicast audio streams.

detaileddescription.para: This will update the metadata of one or more streams.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_UNICAST_CLIENT must be enabled for this function to be enabled.
0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Stop unicast audio streams.

detaileddescription.para: This will stop one or more streams.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_UNICAST_CLIENT must be enabled for this function to be enabled.
0 on success

parameterdescription.para: if a CAP procedure is already in progress
if no state changes will occur

briefdescription.para: Cancel any current Common Audio Profile procedure.

detaileddescription.para: This will stop the current procedure from continuing and making it possible to run a new Common Audio Profile procedure.
It is recommended to do this if any existing procedure takes longer time than expected, which could indicate a missing response from the Common Audio Profile Acceptor.

This does not send any requests to any Common Audio Profile Acceptors involved with the current procedure, and thus notifications from the Common Audio Profile Acceptors may arrive after this has been called. It is thus recommended to either only use this if a procedure has stalled, or wait a short while before starting any new Common Audio Profile procedure after this has been called to avoid getting notifications from the cancelled procedure. The wait time depends on the connection interval, the number of devices in the previous procedure and the behavior of the Common Audio Profile Acceptors.
The respective callbacks of the procedure will be called as part of this with the connection pointer set to 0 and the err value set to -ECANCELED.

briefdescription.para: Create a Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

detaileddescription.para: Create a new audio broadcast source with one or more audio streams.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SOURCE must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Parameters to start the audio streams.
Pointer to the broadcast source created.
param is invalid or broadcast_source is NULL
Could not allocate more broadcast sources, subgroups or ISO streams, or the provided codec configuration data is too large when merging the BIS and subgroup configuration data.
The broadcast source failed to be created for other reasons

briefdescription.para: Start Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

detaileddescription.para: The broadcast source will be visible for scanners once this has been called, and the device will advertise audio announcements.
This will allow the streams in the broadcast source to send audio by calling bt_bap_stream_send().

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SOURCE must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an extended advertising set with periodic advertising configured.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct bt_cap_broadcast_source *broadcast_source, const uint8_t meta[], size_t meta_len)
memberdef.name: bt_cap_initiator_broadcast_audio_update

briefdescription.para: Update broadcast audio streams for a Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SOURCE must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The new metadata. The metadata shall contain a list of CCIDs as well as a non-0 context bitfield.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Stop broadcast audio streams for a Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SOURCE must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The broadcast source to stop. The audio streams in this will be stopped and reset.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Delete Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

detaileddescription.para: This can only be done after the broadcast source has been stopped by calling bt_cap_initiator_broadcast_audio_stop() and after the bt_bap_stream_ops.stopped() callback has been called for all streams in the broadcast source.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR and CONFIG_BT_BAP_BROADCAST_SOURCE must be enabled for this function to be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: The broadcast source to delete. The broadcast_source will be invalidated.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Get the Broadcast Audio Stream Endpoint of a Common Audio Profile broadcast source.

detaileddescription.para: This will encode the BASE of a broadcast source into a buffer, that can be used for advertisement. The encoded BASE will thus be encoded as little-endian. The BASE shall be put into the periodic advertising data (see bt_le_per_adv_set_data()).
See table 3.15 in the Basic Audio Profile v1.0.1 for the structure.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to a buffer where the BASE will be inserted.

simplesect.para: int 0 if on success, errno on error.

briefdescription.para: Iterate through all streams in a broadcast source.

parameterdescription.para: User specified data that is sent to the callback function.
Success (even if no streams exists in the group).
The func returned false and stopped the iteration.

briefdescription.para: Register Common Audio Profile Handover callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure. Shall remain static.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Unregister Common Audio Profile Handover callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure that was previously registered.
cb is NULL or cb was not registered

briefdescription.para: Hands over the sink streams in a unicast group to a broadcast source.

detaileddescription.para: All streams in the provided unicast group will be stopped and released. The sink streams will be tranferred to a broadcast source, and the broadcast source information will be shared with all accepters that are currently receiving audio. Any stream that is not in the streaming state will only be released.
bt_bap_broadcast_assistant_discover() must have been successfully perform for all members in param before starting this procedure.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_CAP_HANDOVER.
0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Hands over the data streams in a broadcast source to a unicast group.

detaileddescription.para: The streams in the broadcast source will be stopped and the broadcast source will be deleted.

simplesect.para: Available only when the following Kconfig option is enabled: CONFIG_BT_CAP_HANDOVER.
0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Register Common Audio Profile Commander callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure. Shall remain static.

briefdescription.para: Unregister Common Audio Profile Commander callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: The callback structure that was previously registered.
cb is NULL or cb was not registered

briefdescription.para: Discovers audio support on a remote device.

detaileddescription.para: This will discover the Common Audio Service (CAS) on the remote device, to verify if the remote device supports the Common Audio Profile.

simplesect.para: CONFIG_BT_CAP_COMMANDER must be enabled for this function. If CONFIG_BT_CAP_INITIATOR is also enabled, it does not matter if bt_cap_commander_discover() or bt_cap_initiator_unicast_discover() is used.

parameterdescription.para: Could not allocated memory for the request

briefdescription.para: Cancel any current Common Audio Profile commander procedure.

detaileddescription.para: This will stop the current procedure from continuing and making it possible to run a new Common Audio Profile procedure.
It is recommended to do this if any existing procedure takes longer time than expected, which could indicate a missing response from the Common Audio Profile Acceptor.

This does not send any requests to any Common Audio Profile Acceptors involved with the current procedure, and thus notifications from the Common Audio Profile Acceptors may arrive after this has been called. It is thus recommended to either only use this if a procedure has stalled, or wait a short while before starting any new Common Audio Profile procedure after this has been called to avoid getting notifications from the cancelled procedure. The wait time depends on the connection interval, the number of devices in the previous procedure and the behavior of the Common Audio Profile Acceptors.
The respective callbacks of the procedure will be called as part of this with the connection pointer set to NULL and the err value set to -ECANCELED.

briefdescription.para: Starts the reception of broadcast audio on one or more remote Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters to start the broadcast audio

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Stops the reception of broadcast audio on one or more remote Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters to stop the broadcast audio

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Distributes the broadcast code on one or more remote Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for distributing the broadcast code

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Change the volume on one or more Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for the volume change

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Change the volume offset on one or more Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for the volume offset change

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Change the volume mute state on one or more Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for the volume mute state change

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Change the microphone mute state on one or more Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for the microphone mute state change

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Change the microphone gain setting on one or more Common Audio Profile Acceptors.

parameterdescription.para: The parameters for the microphone gain setting change

simplesect.para: 0 on success or negative error value on failure.

briefdescription.para: Common Audio Profile (CAP)

detaileddescription.para:
3.2
0.8.0
Common Audio Profile (CAP) provides procedures to start, update, and stop unicast and broadcast Audio Streams on individual or groups of devices using procedures in the Basic Audio Profile (BAP). This profile also provides procedures to control volume and device input on groups of devices using procedures in the Volume Control Profile (VCP) and the Microphone Control Profile (MICP). This profile specification also refers to the Common Audio Service (CAS).
